perturbation.original_text: 10.1 C i s c o P r o d u c t s a r e p r o v i d e d w i t h w r i t t e n l i m i t e d w a r r a n t i e s , a s s e t f o r t h a t t h e f o l l o w i n g U R L : http://www.cisco.com/warp/public/cc/serv/mkt/sup/tsssv/wnty/. Distributor will pass through to Resellers all written limited warranties provided by Cisco with Products purchased by Distributor and Distributor shall contractually require (in whatever manner Distributor deems appropriate) each such Reseller to provide such warranty and other terms to any End User to which the Reseller redistributes the Product.
perturbation.changed_text: 10.1 C i s c o P r o d u c t s a r e p r o v i d e d w i t h w r i t t e n l i m i t e d w a r r a n t i e s , a s s e t f o r t h a t t h e f o l l o w i n g U R L : http://www.cisco.com/warp/public/cc/serv/mkt/sup/tsssv/wnty/. Distributor will pass through to Resellers all written limited warranties provided by Cisco with Products purchased by Distributor and Distributor is not required to provide such warranty and other terms to any End User to which the Reseller redistributes the Product.
perturbation.explanation: The original text in Section 10.1 requires the Distributor to pass on Cisco's warranties to Resellers, who in turn must provide them to End Users. The modified text contradicts this by stating the Distributor isn't required to provide warranties to End Users. This creates an in-text contradiction because, according to the modified clause, the Resellers have no contractual obligation to pass warranties to the End Users.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10.1 C i s c o P r o d u c t s a r e p r o v i d e d w i t h w r i t t e n l i m i t e d w a r r a n t i e s , a s s e t f o r t h a t t h e f o l l o w i n g U R L : http://www.cisco.com/warp/public/cc/serv/mkt/sup/tsssv/wnty/. Distributor will pass through to Resellers all written limited warranties provided by Cisco with Products purchased by Distributor and Distributor is not required to provide such warranty and other terms to any End User to which the Reseller redistributes the Product.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 10.1 mandates that the Distributor contractually obligate Resellers to pass Cisco's warranties to End Users. The changed text directly contradicts this by stating that the Distributor is 'not required to provide such warranty and other terms to any End User'. This creates a clear and direct conflict, rendering the initial obligation unenforceable. The contradicted text, being the changed text itself, highlights the direct in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 18.1 This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and continue thereafter for a period of two (2) years, unless extended by written agreement of both parties or sooner terminated as set forth below. Without prejudice to either party's right to terminate this Agreement as set forth in sub​ sections 18.2 to 18.5 below, Cisco may, by written notice to Distributor, given at least thirty (30) days prior to the end of the then-current term of the Agreement, extend the term of the Agreement for the period set forth in such notice, up to a maximum of one (1) year beyond the then- current expiration date. Any extension shall be on the same terms and conditions then in force, except as may be mutually agreed in writing by the parties. In the event the Agreement expires and Cisco intends to not renew the Agreement, Cisco will provide written notice of same to Distributor. Notwithstanding Cisco's right to extend the term of this Agreement, each party acknowledges that this Agreement shall always be interpreted as being limited in duration to a definite term and that the other party has made no commitments whatsoever regarding the duration or renewal of this Agreement beyond those expressly stated herein.
perturbation.changed_text: 18.1 This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and continue thereafter for a period of two (2) years, unless extended by written agreement of both parties or sooner terminated as set forth below. Without prejudice to either party's right to terminate this Agreement as set forth in sub​ sections 18.2 to 18.5 below, Cisco may, by written notice to Distributor, given at least thirty (30) days prior to the end of the then-current term of the Agreement, extend the term of the Agreement indefinitely, without the Distributor's consent, for the period set forth in such notice. Any extension shall be on the same terms and conditions then in force, except as may be unilaterally modified by Cisco. In the event the Agreement expires and Cisco intends to not renew the Agreement, Cisco will provide written notice of same to Distributor. Notwithstanding Cisco's right to extend the term of this Agreement, each party acknowledges that this Agreement shall always be interpreted as being perpetual and automatically renewing indefinitely.
perturbation.explanation: The original text in section 18.1 states that the agreement lasts for two years, with a possible one-year extension if both parties agree. The modified text contradicts this by allowing Cisco to extend the agreement indefinitely without the Distributor's consent, also stating that Cisco can unilaterally modify the agreement terms, contradicting the need for mutual agreement. Finally, it introduces a complete contradiction by stating the agreement is to be interpreted as 'perpetual and automatically renewing indefinitely', which goes against the original 2 year term with possible 1 year extension
perturbation.contradicted_text: 18.1 This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and continue thereafter for a period of two (2) years, unless extended by written agreement of both parties or sooner terminated as set forth below. Without prejudice to either party's right to terminate this Agreement as set forth in sub​ sections 18.2 to 18.5 below, Cisco may, by written notice to Distributor, given at least thirty (30) days prior to the end of the then-current term of the Agreement, extend the term of the Agreement indefinitely, without the Distributor's consent, for the period set forth in such notice. Any extension shall be on the same terms and conditions then in force, except as may be unilaterally modified by Cisco. In the event the Agreement expires and Cisco intends to not renew the Agreement, Cisco will provide written notice of same to Distributor. Notwithstanding Cisco's right to extend the term of this Agreement, each party acknowledges that this Agreement shall always be interpreted as being perpetual and automatically renewing indefinitely.
perturbation.justification: The modified Section 18.1 introduces a clear contradiction. The original text stipulates a two-year term with a potential one-year extension by mutual agreement. The changed text allows Cisco to extend the agreement indefinitely without the Distributor's consent and to unilaterally modify the agreement terms. Furthermore, the changed text states the agreement is to be interpreted as 'perpetual and automatically renewing indefinitely.' This directly contradicts the initial two-year term and the need for mutual agreement for extensions, creating an in-text contradiction as per Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.
